The girl stands in the extreme foreground on the right side of the frame, her body angled slightly inward as she gazes out at the water. The massive, snow-dusted mountain dominates the middle ground, its peak piercing through the colorful ribbons of the northern lights above. A vast, mirror-like lake fills the center of the composition, perfectly reflecting the cool blues of the sky and the warm glow of the horizon. Soft, wispy clouds drift across the upper sky, blending with the glowing aurora to create a dreamlike canopy. The shoreline where she stands is uneven and textured with small rocks and tufts of wild grass, grounding the ethereal scene.(sweaty, sweatdrop:-2.0) <lora:Anima\anima-turbo-lora-v0.2.safetensors:1.0>

nodes.title: 💡 Tip
widgets_values: You can safely delete this "CivitAI metadata" group if you are not gonna use it. Just don't forget to enable the standard save image node.

nodes.title: 💡Turbo LoRA Sweat fix
widgets_values: Bypass it when you want to include sweat in the final image.

nodes.title: MODEL LINKS
widgets_values:
**text_encoders**

- Anima: [qwen_3_06b_base.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/circlestone-labs/Anima/resolve/main/split_files/text_encoders/qwen_3_06b_base.safetensors) (it is the same as anima_baseV10_txt.safetensors from CivitAI)
- Prompt generator: [qwen3.5_9b_abliterated_mxfp8mixedfp8.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/Winnougan/Qwen-3.5-Abliterated-Comfyui-nvfp4/resolve/main/qwen3.5_9b_abliterated_mxfp8mixedfp8.safetensors?download=true)

**vae**

- [qwen_image_vae.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/circlestone-labs/Anima/resolve/main/split_files/vae/qwen_image_vae.safetensors)

**LoRAs**

- [anima-turbo-lora-v0.2.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/circlestone-labs/Anima-Official-LoRAs/resolve/main/anima-turbo-lora-v0.2.safetensors?download=true)

**ControlNet**

- [anima-lllite-any-test-like-1-step2000.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/kohya-ss/Anima-LLLite/resolve/main/anima-lllite-any-test-like-1-step2000.safetensors?download=true)

**diffusion_models**

- [anima-base-v1.0.safetensors](https://huggingface.co/circlestone-labs/Anima/resolve/main/split_files/diffusion_models/anima-base-v1.0.safetensors?download=true)


## Model Storage Location

```
📂 ComfyUI/
├── 📂 models/
│   ├── 📂 text_encoders/
│   │   └── qwen_3_06b_base.safetensors
│   │   └── qwen3.5_9b_abliterated_mxfp8mixedfp8.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 vae/
│   │   └── qwen_image_vae.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 loras/
│   │   └── anima-turbo-lora-v0.2.safetensors
│   ├── 📂 controlnet/
│   │   └── anima-lllite-any-test-like-1-step2000.safetensors
│   └── 📂 diffusion_models/
│       └── anima-base-v1.0.safetensors
```

nodes.title: Instructions
widgets_values:
**Pess `0` to center the workflow on these instructions any time you need.**

## About Anima

[Anima](https://huggingface.co/circlestone-labs/Anima) (2B-parameter, CircleStone/Comfy Org): Anime/non-photorealistic text-to-image model (no realism).
Limitations: poor realism (intentional), weak high-res/text, plain style.

> **Node color legend**
> | Color | Meaning |
> |-------|---------|
> 🔴 Red | Instructional notes — read-only reference |
> 🟡 Yellow | Configurable elements — adjust to your needs |
> 🟣 Purple | Toggle switches — enable or disable option groups |

---

## 1. Main Settings (press `1`)

### Resolution

Two resolution modes are available. Use the **Fast Groups Bypasser (rgthree)** node to switch between them:

- **Advanced image size** — use this if you are familiar with Resolution Master and want fine-grained control.
- **Simple video size** — recommended for most users; provides a straightforward resolution and image orientation selection.

If the **Upscaler** optional group is enabled, you must also set your desired output resolution in the **`TARGET RESOLUTION (UPSCALER)`** node.

### LoRAs

LoRAs are loaded via the **Power LoRA Loader** node. For each LoRA you wish to use:

1. Select the LoRA file from the dropdown.
2. Adjust the **strength** value to control its influence on the generation.

> Multiple LoRAs can be stacked within the same Power LoRA Loader node.

---

## 2. Prompt (press `2`)

Three prompt input methods are available. Select one based on your use case:

### Option A — Manual Prompt (default)

Enter your prompt directly in the **`POSITIVE PROMPT`** node inside the **Manual prompt** group. This is the standard input method.

### Option B — Image to Prompt (automatic)

Enable the **Image to prompt** group (purple toggle) to automatically generate a prompt from a reference image:

1. Load your reference image into the **`Reference image`** node within this group.
2. Optionally, adjust the **seed** inside the **`Prompt generator`** subgraph to explore different prompt variations.
3. To preview a generated prompt before committing to a full run, **partially execute the workflow up to the `Generated prompt` node**.
4. Once satisfied, run the full workflow.

> **Tip:** To iterate further after generation — copy the generated prompt text, disable the **Image to prompt** group, paste the prompt into the **Manual prompt** group, and edit as needed.

---

## 3. Sampling Settings *(optional)*  (press `3`)

The default values for this group are production-ready and do not require adjustment for typical use. The following parameters can be modified if needed:

- **CFG scale** — controls how strictly the model follows the prompt.
- **Sampling steps** — number of denoising iterations.
- **Sampler / Scheduler** — algorithm used for the diffusion process.

### Turbo Mode

**Turbo mode** is a sub-option within this group, controlled by a purple toggle switch.

When enabled, Turbo mode:

- Overrides the base sampling settings with optimized turbo parameters.
- Automatically loads the **Turbo LoRA** (`anima-turbo-lora-v0.2`).
- Reduces the required sampling steps from **30+** down to approximately **10**.
- Results in significantly faster generation at a minor quality trade-off.

> Turbo mode is recommended for rapid iteration and previewing.

---

## 4. ControlNet  (press `4`)

ControlNet allows you to guide the generation using a reference image for pose, depth, or scene composition.

1. Load your reference image into the **`Reference image`** node within the ControlNet group.
2. Select the desired **ControlNet type** from the available options (e.g., pose, depth, canny, etc.).

> **Note:** The ControlNet reference image and the Image-to-Prompt reference image serve different purposes and are intentionally separate inputs. You may use the same image for both, or use a different image for each — for example, using one image to dictate composition while using another to define the visual style for prompt generation.

---

## 5. Sampling   (press `5`)

The **Sampling** group contains the primary generation seed.

- Change the **seed value** to produce different results from the same prompt and settings.
- Use a fixed seed for reproducible outputs, or randomize for variation.

---

## 6. Optional Groups   (press `5`)

The following groups are disabled by default and can be enabled via their respective purple toggle switches.

### Upscaler

Performs post-generation upscaling using **Nvidia Video Super Resolution**.

- Enable this group if you require a higher-resolution final output than what is natively generated.
- Ensure the target resolution is set in the **`TARGET RESOLUTION (UPSCALER)`** node.

### Color Correction

Applies basic post-processing adjustments to the final image:

- **Brightness**
- **Contrast**
- **Saturation**

Enable this group if the generated output requires tonal correction before saving.

---

## 7. CivitAI Metadata  (press `6`)

This group automatically embeds **CivitAI-compatible metadata** into the saved output image, including generation parameters such as prompt, seed, sampler, and model information.

- The workflow functions correctly with or without this group active.
- If you do not intend to upload outputs to CivitAI, this group can be safely **deleted** with no impact on generation quality or workflow behavior.

nodes.title: NEGATIVE PROMPT
widgets_values: worst quality, low quality, score_1, score_2, score_3, blurry, jpeg artifacts, sepia

nodes.title: POSITIVE PROMPT
widgets_values:
masterpiece, best quality, score_9, year 2024, absurdres, safe, 1girl, long black hair, white and pink yukata, dark red obi, black geta sandals, floral hair ornament, standing pose, looking away, serene expression, aurora borealis, vibrant blue sky, snow-capped mountain, calm lake, reflection, lush greenery, rocky shore, wild grass, soft clouds, ethereal atmosphere
The girl stands in the extreme foreground on the right side of the frame, her body angled slightly inward as she gazes out at the water. The massive, snow-dusted mountain dominates the middle ground, its peak piercing through the colorful ribbons of the northern lights above. A vast, mirror-like lake fills the center of the composition, perfectly reflecting the cool blues of the sky and the warm glow of the horizon. Soft, wispy clouds drift across the upper sky, blending with the glowing aurora to create a dreamlike canopy. The shoreline where she stands is uneven and textured with small rocks and tufts of wild grass, grounding the ethereal scene.

You are an expert prompt engineer specialized in the Anima 1.0 image generation model. 
Your sole task is to analyze a reference image and output a single, highly accurate generation prompt. The prompt will be fed directly into a CLIP encoder.

## OUTPUT FORMAT RULES (CRITICAL)
- Output ONLY the final prompt text. 
- The first character of your response must be the first character of the prompt.
- Do NOT write conversational filler (e.g., "Here is the prompt", "Sure", "This image depicts", "The prompt is...").
- Do NOT wrap the output in quotes, code blocks, or markdown.
- Do NOT output section headers or JSON.
- Do NOT include negative prompt content of any kind (e.g., no "worst quality", "low quality", "bad anatomy").

## ANIMA 1.0 SYNTAX & TAGGING RULES
- All Danbooru-style tags must be lowercase.
- Use spaces instead of underscores in tags (e.g., "brown hair" not "brown_hair").
  - Exception: Score tags use underscores (e.g., score_9, score_8).
- Artist tags must be prefixed with @ (e.g., "@artist name"). 
- Never use prompt weighting syntax (e.g., ((tag)), [tag], (tag:1.2)). It is ineffective for Anima.
- For non-anime painterly or illustration styles ONLY, prepend "ye-pop" or "deviantart" on its own line before the prompt. Do not use for anime styles.

## MANDATORY HYBRID STRUCTURE
You MUST structure your prompt in two distinct parts, separated by exactly two newlines (a blank line). 

PART 1: THE TAG BLOCK
A comma-separated list establishing the foundation. It must strictly follow this order:
1. Quality/Meta: masterpiece, best quality, score_9, year 2024, absurdres, etc.
2. Safety: Include exactly one (safe, sensitive, nsfw, explicit).
3. Core Identity: Subject count (e.g., 1girl, no humans), character name, franchise, artist tag (@).
4. Visual Traits: Specific appearance, clothing, accessories, and distinct object tags.

[DOUBLE NEWLINE]

PART 2: THE NATURAL LANGUAGE BLOCK (CRITICAL)
You MUST break out of comma-separated tagging here. You must write 3 to 5 complete, grammatically correct English sentences using verbs, subjects, and periods. 
- DO NOT just list traits. 
- Describe the exact spatial relationships, framing, and composition. 
- Placement: Where are subjects/objects located in relation to each other and the camera? (e.g., "The character is positioned in the extreme foreground to the left, leaning against a crumbling brick wall. The camera is positioned at a high angle, looking down.")
- Action & Interaction: How do elements interact physically or visually? 
- Lighting & Atmosphere: Describe the environment, depth of field, and lighting behavior in full sentences (e.g., "Harsh volumetric lighting casts long, dramatic shadows across the stone floor.").

## ANALYSIS CHECKLIST
When analyzing the reference image, ensure your final output captures:
- Exact subject count and identifiable canonical traits.
- Accurate mapping of clothing materials and textures.
- The precise framing, perspective, and background geometry in the natural language section.
Anima 1.0 Base for the PC master race [SFW / NSFW] - Image to prompt + Turbo mode + ControlNet + 4k upscaler + CivitAI medatada
